DISCOVERY 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA GARCIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT MARGIE ANN THOMPSON'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (CCP 2030.300, 2030.310) - THE COURT ORDERED THE DEFENDANT TO VERIFY ALL AMENDED RESPONSES IF SHE HAS NOT DONE SO. THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING ON ALL OF THE THREE MOTIONS: AS TO FORM INTERROOGATORIES IT IS GRANTED AS TO 12.3 AND 17.1 AND DENIED AS TO 17.1. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IT IS GRANTED AS TO 24, 51-53; 91, 92 AND 98; DENIED AS TO 94-97; AS TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IT IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 1, 11 AND 15 WHICH PERTAINS TO THE REVENUE VEHICLE REPORT, STATEMENT OF MS. THOMPSON OBTAINED IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION WHICH IS PROTECTED BY THE ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE AND ALL PHOTOGRAPHS, FILMS THAT RELATE TO SUBJECT INCIDENT. GRANTED AS TO 24, 31, 47-48, 52-53, 58 AND 63. DENIED AS TO 25, 35, 54-56, 57, 66-67. THE COURT AWARDED SANCTIONS FOR THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $820.00 FOR ALL THREE MOTIONS. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Jul-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHAANA A. RAHMAN, TEL. NO. 956-9245 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KAREN E. KRIBY, CHIEF OF TORT LITIGATION, TEL. NO. 554-3891 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA GARCIA'S Motion To Compel Defendant Margie Ann Thompson's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One and Request For Monetary Sanctions (Ccp 2030.300, 2030.310) - THE COURT ORDERED THE DEFENDANT TO VERIFY ALL AMENDED RESPONSES IF SHE HAS NOT DONE SO. THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING ON ALL OF THE THREE MOTIONS: AS TO FORM INTERROOGATORIES IT IS GRANTED AS TO 12.3 AND 17.1 AND DENIED AS TO 17.1. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IT IS GRANTED AS TO 24, 51-53; 91, 92 AND 98; DENIED AS TO 94-97; AS TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IT IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 1, 11 AND 15 WHICH PERTAINS TO THE REVENUE VEHICLE REPORT, STATEMENT OF MS. THOMPSON OBTAINED IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION WHICH IS PROTECTED BY THE ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE AND ALL PHOTOGRAPHS, FILMS THAT RELATE TO SUBJECT INCIDENT. GRANTED AS TO 24, 31, 47-48, 52-53, 58 AND 63. DENIED AS TO 25, 35, 54-56, 57, 66-67. THE COURT AWARDED SANCTIONS FOR THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $820.00 FOR ALL THREE MOTIONS. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported =301/PJB
